meta.title: Prognostic Factors and Longitudinal Change in Long-Term Outcome of Frontal Lobe Epilepsy Surgery
meta.authors: Xu C, Yu T, Zhang G, et al.
meta.outcome: Prognostic Factors and Longitudinal Change in Long-Term Outcome of Frontal Lobe Epilepsy Surgery
meta.clinical_utility: We aimed to investigate the possible predictive factors and longitudinal change in long-term surgical outcome after refractory frontal lobe epilepsy surgery. We retrospectively reviewed 82 patients who underwent frontal lobe resection. Invasive monitoring was performed in 43 patients.

model.selector_info: Selecting 'Yes' indicates that the patient underwent a frontal lobectomy as part of the surgical treatment for refractory frontal lobe epilepsy. Based on the study, the probability of seizure freedom was 57.3% at 1 year, 51.2% at 2 years, and 50.0% at 5 years postoperatively. Favorable outcomes were associated with factors such as the presence of frontal focal lesions on MRI and ictal frontal rhythms on EEG. However, acute postoperative seizures (APOS) and generalized ictal discharges were predictive of poorer outcomes. The study included 82 patients with refractory frontal lobe epilepsy, aged 2.5 to 54.8 years at surgery, with various resection types. The most common histopathological findings included focal cortical dysplasia (39.0%), tumors (9.8%), and scar lesions (15.9%).
